concerns.text: Task T008 mandates 'attempt fallback public search (Zenodo)' if the verified URL is missing. This violates the Plan (Data Acquisition Strategy) and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility), which strictly require the system to 'terminate with error code DATA_SOURCE_MISSING' if no verified URL exists. Fallback to unverified sources silently relaxes the reproducibility constraint.

Task T023 mandates calculating VIF for 'ALL models (RF, GB) using surrogate linear modeling'. This contradicts the Plan (Statistical Validation Details) which explicitly restricts VIF diagnostics to the Linear Regression baseline only, stating VIF is 'NOT used to validate the Random Forest or Gradient Boosting models'. This introduces an unapproved statistical procedure.

Task T027 describes sensitivity analysis as sweeping 'α over a range of low values'. This violates FR-008 and the Spec (User Story 3) which strictly define the sweep range as the discrete set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}. 'Range of low values' is an undefined semantic relaxation that allows arbitrary thresholds not authorized by the requirement.

Tasks T024 and T026 instruct the system to 'run' permutation and bootstrap tests with a 'WARNING' log level if N < 50. This contradicts the Plan (Statistical Validation Details) and Spec (Edge Cases) which explicitly state that if N < 50, these tests must be 'skipped' entirely and a specific 'Insufficient Power' flag raised. Running tests on insufficient data violates the statistical rigor constraint.
